description: Index 'posthog_persondistinctid_distinct_id_team_id_aac1d8f8' on table 'posthog_persondistinctid' has not been used in the last 21 days (0 scans, 1.4GB storage). Dropping it would remove write-amplification overhead on a table with high INSERT and UPDATE volume from person merging. Detected by the 'index_unused' check.

Sequential scan on 'posthog_propertydefinition' accounts for 8.3% of total runtime. Filter clause is `WHERE project_id = $1 AND query @@ to_tsquery($2)`. The Index Advisor suggests adding a composite GIN index on `posthog_propertydefinition(project_id, query)` to reduce average query time from ~620ms to ~9ms based on plan estimates. Property-definition lookups dominate taxonomy autocomplete latency.

description: Autovacuum on 'posthog_eventdefinition' is significantly behind. The dead_tuple_ratio is 41% and the table has not been vacuumed in 7 days. This is causing index bloat (estimated 2.8GB wasted) and slowing down range scans of event definitions when populating the taxonomy panel. Consider lowering autovacuum_vacuum_scale_factor for this table — definitions are upserted on every new event property seen, so the default scale factor is too conservative.
